steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Find the radius using the given distance PO
steps.content: Since O is the center of the semicircle and NP is its diameter, O is the midpoint of NP. This means PO equals the radius. Given PO = 52 feet, we have radius = 52 feet and diameter NP = 104 feet.

steps.title: Use the midpoint condition to find the rectangle width
steps.content: P is the midpoint of NQ, which means the segment NP equals the segment PQ. Since NP = 104 feet, we have PQ = 104 feet, so the rectangle width is NQ = 104 + 104 = 208 feet.

steps.title: Calculate the total area as semicircle plus rectangle
steps.content:
Semicircle area =
$$\frac{1}{2}\pi r^2 = \frac{1}{2}\pi(52)^2 = \frac{1}{2}\pi(2704) = 1352\pi$$
Rectangle area =
$$208 \times 18 = 3744$$
Total area =
$$1352\pi + 3744$$
Therefore $a = 1352$ and $b = 3744$, giving us $a - b = 1352 - 3744 = -2392$.
